_meta.title: Experiment 6 real corridor configuration
_meta.purpose: Three real Thai corridors for fuel-aware route optimization evaluation.
_meta.project: Fuel-Aware Route Optimization on Thailand Road Networks (Wai Yan Aung & Prajwal Bhandary, MSc CS, AIT)

price_snapshot.source: Thailand Energy Policy and Planning Office (EPPO) via PTT/Bangchak retail postings
notes: Gasohol 91 chosen because it is the most common retail fuel for private vehicles and is the price series EPPO publishes daily with provincial variants.

Price snapshot is anchored to the April 3, 2026 PTT/Bangchak pump price for Bangkok. Provincial prices below approximate documented EPPO provincial dispersion (~1-3 THB/L spread, higher in far-north and far-south where transport costs are greater).
Do not mix results from this snapshot with a rerun under a different snapshot. If rerunning on a different date, regenerate this file.

vehicle_rationale.tank_capacity_l: 40 L matches the plan's fixed parameter and a typical Thai compact sedan (e.g. Honda City, Toyota Yaris).
vehicle_rationale.initial_fuel_l: 28 L = 0.7 * tank, the same 70% starting fraction used throughout the synthetic experiments.
vehicle_rationale.efficiency_km_per_l: 10 km/L is a conservative mixed urban/highway figure for the vehicle class and matches Chapter 3 of the final report.

corridor_rationale: Bangkok -> Chiang Mai (~700 km) covers the country's busiest north-south corridor and follows Highway 1 (Phahonyothin) -> Highway 32 -> Highway 11.
Bangkok -> Phuket (~840 km) is the longest corridor, following Highway 4 (Phetkasem) south through Chumphon and Phang Nga. Tests the algorithm at the range upper bound.
Bangkok -> Khon Kaen (~450 km) is the medium-length northeastern corridor via Highway 1 -> Highway 2 (Mittraphap) at the Saraburi km-107 junction. Tests behaviour at a shorter corridor.
_meta.anchor_point_rationale: Endpoints are pinned to documented highway start/end points (not abstract city centres) so OSMnx resolves them to stable road-graph nodes across reruns.

defaults._notes: fallback_price equals the Bangkok base price from _meta.price_snapshot. Stations whose province cannot be resolved will use this value, which is documented as the source in the generated instance.

corridors.label: Bangkok to Chiang Mai
corridors._route_description: Highway 1 (Phahonyothin) north from Victory Monument; Highway 32 from Bang Pa-in; rejoin Highway 1 at Nakhon Sawan; Highway 11 from In Buri/Sing Buri junction through Lampang to Chiang Mai.

source.name: Victory Monument, Bangkok
source.description: Historical starting point of Highway 1 (Phahonyothin Road) at the junction of Phaya Thai and Ratchathewi districts.

target.name: Chiang Mai (Highway 11 terminus)
target.description: Eastern entry to Chiang Mai city where Highway 11 (Super Highway) terminates.

corridors.label: Bangkok to Phuket
corridors._route_description: Highway 35 (Rama II) south from Bangkok; Highway 4 (Phetkasem) through Ratchaburi, Phetchaburi, Prachuap Khiri Khan, Chumphon, Ranong, Phang Nga; Sarasin Bridge to Phuket Island.

source.name: Bang Khae / Phetkasem entry, Bangkok
source.description: Southern anchor of Highway 4 (Phetkasem Road) at Bang Khae district where Phetkasem begins its southbound run out of the Bangkok metropolitan area.

target.name: Phuket Town
target.description: Phuket town centre, terminus of Highway 4 after Sarasin Bridge crossing onto Phuket Island.

corridors.label: Bangkok to Khon Kaen
corridors._route_description: Highway 1 (Phahonyothin) north from Victory Monument to the Saraburi interchange at km 107; turn onto Highway 2 (Mittraphap) through Nakhon Ratchasima to Khon Kaen. Route documented by the Tourism Authority of Thailand and Highway 2 Wikipedia entry.

source.name: Victory Monument, Bangkok
source.description: Same Highway 1 anchor as the Chiang Mai corridor for consistency.

target.name: Khon Kaen (Mittraphap Road)
target.description: Khon Kaen city centre on Highway 2 (Mittraphap Road).
target.osm_hint: Mittraphap Road at Khon Kaen city
